summary: Decompiled three game-mode functions in gm_16F1, gm_1BA8, and gmregclear, replacing placeholder comments with C implementations and updating related header prototypes. The PR body claims fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for all three target functions, and an automated GALE01 report records the three target functions as new 100% matches while also showing unrelated match-score movement and two .sdata2 regressions.

key_files.role: Implemented fn_80170110 as an unlock-table lookup/check using lbl_803D5A4C entries, mask/flag testing, pl_80039418 for standard kinds, and fn_801701C0 for higher kinds.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Summary and Unlock check description

key_files.role: Updated function prototypes for fn_80170110 and fn_801701C0 from unknown placeholders toward concrete return and parameter types.
Implemented gm_801BEDA8 as CSS match-type cycling with wraparound and synchronization of CSS data into VsModeData.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Match type cycling on CSS description

key_files.role: Implemented fn_8017F09C as clear bonus calculation gated by clear/eligibility state, multiplying timer_seconds by data->x108 and clamping negative product to 0.
Updated fn_8017F09C prototype from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to s32 fn_8017F09C(void).

what_changed: Replaced the fn_80170110 placeholder in gm_16F1.c with a loop over lbl_803D5A4C entries, a sentinel kind check against 0x29A, an unlock flag mask check, and dispatch to pl_80039418 or fn_801701C0 depending on kind.
Changed gm_16F1.h prototypes for fn_80170110 and fn_801701C0 to concrete int-returning signatures with void* and integer parameters.

Replaced the gm_801BEDA8 placeholder in gm_1BA8.c with logic that obtains CSSData and VsModeData, decrements or increments css->match_type with wraparound over 24 values, then copies css->data into *vs.
Replaced the fn_8017F09C placeholder in gmregclear.c with eligibility checks over lbl_80472D28 and gm_8016AE38 state, then a timer_seconds * data->x108 calculation with negative results clamped to 0.
Changed gmregclear.h prototype for fn_8017F09C to s32 fn_8017F09C(void).
smart_moves: Updated owning headers alongside recovered function bodies, reducing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or UNK_T use where the new bodies supported concrete signatures.

Used existing domain structs and fields visible in the diff, such as CSSData, VsModeData, MinorScene, lbl_8046B6A0_t, timer_seconds, and bitfield-like members, instead of raw pointer arithmetic in the changed code.
Expressed CSS match-type wraparound with readable C control flow and modulo for forward cycling rather than preserving a placeholder or generated-looking branch-only form.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a decompiled body proves parameter and return types, update the owning header in the same PR so call surfaces move away from UNK_RET, UNK_PARAMS, and UNK_T.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_16F1.h changed fn_80170110 and fn_801701C0 prototypes

decomp_lessons.lesson: For small game-mode state helpers, typed local variables and known struct fields can produce compact source that matches while remaining reviewable.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_1BA8.c uses CSSData, VsModeData, pending_scene_change, match_type
raw_diff: gmregclear.c uses lbl_8046B6A0_t, x24C8 fields, timer_seconds

decomp_lessons.lesson: Automated score reports should be read narrowly: target-function 100% matches can coexist with unrelated section or fuzzy-score movement that may need separate triage.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: GALE01 report records target gm functions as 100% new matches
human_pr_text: GALE01 report also records unrelated improvements and .sdata2 regressions

naming_conventions.convention: Address-style names were retained for functions and unknown global structs where semantic meaning was not established in the PR, including fn_80170110, fn_8017F09C, lbl_803D5A4C_t, and lbl_80472D28_t.
Short local role names were used for obvious temporary roles, such as css, vs, data, p, p2, curr, flags, product, and ret.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: fn_80170110 uses a while loop over table entries plus a goto label to share the final flag-check path when the sentinel kind 0x29A is reached.
gm_801BEDA8 implements reverse wraparound with an explicit zero check assigning 0x17, while forward wraparound is expressed as (match_type + 1) % 24.

fn_8017F09C preserves a separate flag local and duplicate gm_8016AE38 call inside the guarded block, which may reflect matching-sensitive control flow or register/lifetime shape, but the PR evidence does not explain why this shape was required.
The PR body claims fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for all three target functions, and the GALE01 report lists fn_8017F09C, gm_801BEDA8, and fn_80170110 as 100.00% after the PR.

review_feedback.source: automated GALE01 issue comment by encounter
review_feedback.feedback: Matched code increased to 59.10% (+0.02%, +880 bytes), with the three target gm functions reported as new 100.00% matches.

review_feedback.source: automated GALE01 issue comment by encounter
review_feedback.feedback: The report also listed two regressions in unmatched .sdata2 items: main/melee/gm/gmregclear .sdata2 from 30.12% to 24.43% and main/melee/it/items/itclimbersice .sdata2 from 66.67% to 50.00%. No human review comment in the loaded slice explains these regressions.

follow_up_queries: fn_80170110 unlock table lbl_803D5A4C kind flags 0x29A
fn_8017F09C timer_seconds x108 x118 x11A x11B x24C8
gm_16F1.h fn_801701C0 prototype int void* int int

notes: Changed files and code edits are strongly evidenced by the raw diff and changed_files list.
Verification is evidenced by the PR body claim and an automated GALE01 report, but there is no loaded local build log or objdiff transcript beyond fuzzy/match report text.
No review comments or formal human review records are present in the loaded slice.
The PR body includes a Claude Code generation note; do not infer authored intent beyond the code and report evidence.

The automated report includes unrelated matches/improvements and two .sdata2 regressions, but the loaded evidence does not explain whether those were accepted, investigated, or incidental fuzzy-report noise.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recover header prototypes when function bodies prove signatures
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2324 decompiled gm functions and updated gm_16F1.h and gmregclear.h prototypes from unknown placeholders to concrete signatures supported by the recovered bodies.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Treat automated report side effects separately from target matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2324's report shows target gm functions as 100% matches while also listing unrelated improvements and .sdata2 regressions; intake should preserve this distinction rather than treating all score movement as intentional PR work.

source_update_candidates.title: Document narrow handling of automated fuzzy reports with unrelated section movement
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding guidance that automated match reports for focused decomp PRs may include unrelated fuzzy improvements/regressions; accepted match claims should identify the target functions, while unrelated section movement should be recorded as regression triage rather than inferred author intent.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: GALE01 report for PR #2324 lists three target gm 100% matches plus unrelated improvements and .sdata2 regressions
source_update_candidates.reason: This PR provides a clear example where target function matches and report side effects need separate curation.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the PR body's natural-language descriptions as verified game behavior beyond what the diff and report support.
Do not infer that the .sdata2 regressions were acceptable or intentional; the loaded slice contains no human review discussion explaining them.
Do not generalize the specific goto in fn_80170110 or duplicate gm_8016AE38 call in fn_8017F09C as preferred style; the PR evidence does not explain those shapes beyond the claimed match.
